title: Overseer HALT-ALL marker
description:
Per-user halt marker that pauses every /auto-optimize loop across every repo in the GuitarAlchemist ecosystem. Lives at $env:USERPROFILE/.demerzel/HALT-ALL (Windows) or ~/.demerzel/HALT-ALL (Unix). Presence == halted; absence == not halted. The file body is metadata for human readers and audit; the gate-trip is the file's existence.

Produced by: Demerzel ACP `/halt POST` endpoint, MCP tool `demerzel:halt-all`, or operator hand-edit.

Consumed by: per-repo loop runners (e.g. /auto-optimize Step 0, Scripts/dev-process-overseer.ps1) BEFORE proceeding to any cycle. Opportunistic check — if the file does not exist OR the directory is unreadable, the consumer falls back to per-repo `state/.loop-halted` per the offline-fallback decision (D-offline-fallback in docs/plans/2026-05-16-arch-demerzel-overseer-extension-plan.md).

schema_version.description: Bump on breaking changes to this schema. Consumers MUST reject markers with unknown schema_version.

halted_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the marker was written.

halted_by.description: Identifier of the operator or agent that wrote the marker. Free-form for v1 (e.g. 'operator:spareilleux', 'agent:claude-code-2.1.126', 'demerzel-acp').

reason.description: Short human-readable reason for the halt. Shown to agents that abort. Examples: 'Investigating runaway cost burn', 'Pause before quarterly release freeze', 'Operator paged — testing rollback'.

scope.description: What the halt covers. 'loops-only' (default) = /auto-optimize and similar cycle drivers; 'loops-and-batch' = also CI-side autonomous batch jobs; 'global' = everything including manual /digest writes. Per D-halt-scope, v1 ships with 'loops-only' as the only honored value.

expires_at.description: Optional RFC3339 UTC timestamp after which the halt is no longer in effect. Null = halt persists until manually lifted. Consumers MUST check expires_at and treat an expired marker as absent.

exempt_agents.description: Optional list of agent identifiers that can ignore this halt (e.g. when halting all loops EXCEPT one debugging session). Empty or absent = no exemptions.

examples.reason: Investigating cost burn on Mistral teacher rollouts
Scheduled freeze: mobile release branch cut
